blocks:
Surah Al-Ahqaf

Meccan, and it is 35 verses

Surah Al-Ahqaf

Meccan, the number of its verses is thirty-five verses (Kufan count), four in the remaining (counts).

بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(١) حم. (1) Ḥā Mīm. (٢) تَنْزِيلُ الْكِتابِ مِنَ اللهِ الْعَزِيزِ الْحَكِيمِ. (2) The sending down of the Book is from Allah, the All-mighty, the All-wise. (٣) ما خَلَقْنَا السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضَ وَما بَيْنَهُما إِلَّا بِالْحَقِّ وَأَجَلٍ مُسَمًّى ** which everything terminates at, and that is the Day of Resurrection, or (it refers to) each one (of them), which is the end of its appointed duration of existence وَالَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا عَمَّا أُنْذِرُوا مُعْرِضُونَ ** They do not reflect upon it, nor do they prepare for its arrival.

(٤) قُلْ أَرَأَيْتُمْ ما تَدْعُونَ مِنْ دُونِ اللهِ أَرُونِي ما ذا خَلَقُوا مِنَ الْأَرْضِ أَمْ لَهُمْ شِرْكٌ فِي السَّماواتِ ** (4) Say, 'Tell me, what about those whom you invoke besides Allah? Show me what they have created of the earth. Do they have any share in the heavens?' Meaning: Inform [Me] about the state of your deities after reflecting upon them. Is it conceivable that they inherently have any involvement in the creation of anything from the parts of the universe, such that they would deserve worship thereby? ائْتُونِي بِكِتابٍ مِنْ قَبْلِ هذا ** Bring me a scripture [revealed] before this Before this Scripture, meaning the Quran, for it speaks clearly of monotheism (التوحيد). أَوْ أَثارَةٍ مِنْ عِلْمٍ ** or a vestige of knowledge, Or a remnant of knowledge that has remained with you from the sciences of the ancients. Is there anything in it that indicates their deserving of worship or the command for it? إِنْ كُنْتُمْ صادِقِينَ ** if you are truthful.' In your claim. And this is an obligation [upon them] to produce evidence that indicates their divinity (ألوهيّتهم) in any way, based on transmission (نقلا), following the obligation [upon them] to produce evidence that necessitates it based on reason (عقلا). And in al-Majma': ‘Ali (peace be upon him) read: أو أثره (aw atharah) with a sukūn on the thā' (الثاء) and without an alif. In al-Kāfī, narrated from al-Bāqir (peace be upon him): He was asked about this āyah. He said: "By the Scripture (الكتاب), He meant the Torah (التوراة) and the Gospel (الإنجيل). As for 'a vestige of knowledge' (أَثارَةٍ مِنْ عِلْمٍ), He only meant by that the knowledge of the successors (أوصياء) of the Prophets."

(٥) وَمَنْ أَضَلُّ مِمَّنْ يَدْعُوا مِنْ دُونِ اللهِ مَنْ لا يَسْتَجِيبُ لَهُ ** (And who is more astray than him who invokes, besides Allah, those who will not answer him) [This is] a denial that anyone could be more astray than the polytheists, since they abandoned the worship of the All-Hearing (السميع), the Responder (المجيب), the All-Powerful (القادر), the All-Aware (الخبير), for the worship of those who would not respond to them even if they heard their supplication, let alone knowing their secrets (سرائرهم) and looking after their interests (مصالحهم). إِلى يَوْمِ الْقِيامَةِ ** (until the Day of Resurrection) [meaning] as long as the world lasts. وَهُمْ عَنْ دُعائِهِمْ غافِلُونَ ** (and they are oblivious of their prayers) because they are either inanimate objects (جمادات) or subjugated servants (عباد مسخّرون) preoccupied with their own states (أحوالهم).

(٦) وَإِذا حُشِرَ النَّاسُ كانُوا لَهُمْ أَعْداءً ** (6) When mankind are mustered, they will be their enemies, They will harm them and not benefit them. وَكانُوا بِعِبادَتِهِمْ كافِرِينَ ** and they will disavow their worship. Each of the two pronouns (الضميرين) has two possible referents (ذو وجهين). (٧) وَإِذا تُتْلى عَلَيْهِمْ آياتُنا بَيِّناتٍ قالَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا لِلْحَقِ ** (7) When Our manifest signs are recited to them, the faithless say of the truth concerning it (لأجله) and regarding its matter (وفي شأنه) ** لَمَّا جاءَهُمْ هذا سِحْرٌ مُبِينٌ ** when it comes to them, ‘This is manifest magic.’ whose falsehood (بطلانه) is apparent (ظاهر). (٨) أَمْ يَقُولُونَ افْتَراهُ ** (8) Do they say, ‘He has fabricated it’? This is a shift (اضراب) from mentioning their naming it magic (سحراً) to mentioning what is more heinous than that, and a denial of it (وإنكار له), and an expression of astonishment (وتعجيب). قُلْ إِنِ افْتَرَيْتُهُ ** Say, ‘If I have fabricated it,’ hypothetically (على الفرض) فَلا تَمْلِكُونَ لِي مِنَ اللهِ شَيْئاً ** you have no power to help me against Allah. meaning: If Allah were to hasten punishment upon me (عاجلني الله بالعقوبة), you would not be able to ward off any part of it. So how could I dare (اجترئ) to do so and expose myself to punishment without expecting any benefit or warding off any harm from your side? هُوَ أَعْلَمُ بِما تُفِيضُونَ فِيهِ ** He knows best what you are engrossed in. meaning: what you plunge into (تندفعون فيه) regarding the disparagement (القدح) of His signs. ** كَفى بِهِ شَهِيداً بَيْنِي وَبَيْنَكُمْ ** He is sufficient as a witness between me and you, He bears witness for me regarding the conveyance (بالبلاغ) and against you regarding the lie (بالكذب) and the denial (والإنكار). And this is a threat (وعيد) regarding the recompense for their plunging into it (إفاضتهم). وَهُوَ الْغَفُورُ الرَّحِيمُ ** and He is the All-forgiving, the All-merciful.’ This is a promise (وعد) of forgiveness and mercy for whoever repents and believes, and an indication (اشعار) of Allah's forbearance (حلم) toward them despite their audacity (جرأتهم). A narration (حديث) concerning the occasion of the revelation (شأن نزول) of this verse has previously appeared in al-Uyun in Surat ash-Shura regarding the Almighty's saying: وَهُوَ الَّذِي يَقْبَلُ التَّوْبَةَ عَنْ عِبادِهِ. and it is He who accepts the repentance of His servants.

(٩) قُلْ ما كُنْتُ بِدْعاً مِنَ الرُّسُلِ ** (9) Say, 'I am not a novelty among the apostles, (I am not) an unprecedented one among them, calling you to what they did not call to, or being capable of what they were not capable of. ** وَما أَدْرِي ما يُفْعَلُ بِي وَلا بِكُمْ ** nor do I know what will be done with me or with you. in the two abodes (الدارين) in detail, since I have no knowledge of the unseen (الغيب). And a narrative concerning argumentation (الاحتجاج) regarding this verse has already preceded in the Sixth Introduction (المقدّمة السادسة). ** إِنْ أَتَّبِعُ إِلَّا ما يُوحى إِلَيَ ** I follow only what is revealed to me, I do not exceed it. ** وَما أَنَا إِلَّا نَذِيرٌ ** and I am only a manifest warner.' concerning the punishment of Allah, ** مُبِينٌ ** clarifying the warning concerning the consequences by means of clear proofs (الشواهد المبيّنة) and confirming miracles (المعجزات المصدّقة).

(١٠) قُلْ أَرَأَيْتُمْ إِنْ كَانَ مِنْ عِنْدِ اللَّهِ ** (10) Say, 'Tell me, if it is from Allah أي القرآن ** وَكَفَرْتُمْ بِهِ وَشَهِدَ شَاهِدٌ مِنْ بَنِي إِسْرَائِيلَ ** meaning the Quran, and you disbelieve in it, and a witness from the Children of Israel testifies to its conformity [with the Torah], قيل هو عبد الله بن سلام وقيل موسى عليه السلام وشهادته ما في التوراة من نعت الرسول صلى الله عليه وآله ** عَلَى مِثْلِهِ ** It is said that he is Abdullah ibn Salam (عبد الله بن سلام), and it is also said that he is Moses (موسى) (peace be upon him). His testimony is what is found in the Torah concerning the description (نعت) of the Messenger (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace), concerning that which is similar to it (مِثْلِهِ) ممّا في التوراة من المعاني المصدقة له المطابقة عليه ** فَآمَنَ ** in the Torah, regarding the meanings (المعاني) that confirm it and correspond to it, and he believes [in it], أي بالقرآن لمّا رآه من جنس الوحي مطابقاً للحقّ وَاسْتَكْبَرْتُمْ عنِ الايمان ** إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَا يَهْدِي الْقَوْمَ الظَّالِمِينَ ** meaning, he believed in the Quran when he saw that it was of the same kind as the Revelation (الوحي), corresponding to the truth, while you were arrogant (وَاسْتَكْبَرْتُمْ) regarding belief, while you are disdainful? Indeed Allah does not guide the wrongdoing lot.' استيناف مشعر بأنّ كفرهم به لضلالهم المسبّب عن ظلمهم ودليل على الجواب المحذوف اي ألستم ظالمين. This is a new sentence (استيناف) indicating that their disbelief in it is due to their misguidance (ضلالهم) caused by their wrongdoing (ظلمهم), and it serves as evidence for the omitted answer [to the question], meaning: Are you not wrongdoers?

(١١) ** وَقالَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا لِلَّذِينَ آمَنُوا ** (11) And the faithless said of the faithful concerning them لَوْ كانَ خَيْراً ** (Had it been any good,) meaning: the faith (الإيمان) or that which Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله brought ما سَبَقُونا إِلَيْهِ ** ('they would not have preceded us to it.') while they are poor, clients (موال), and callers (دعاة). وَإِذْ لَمْ يَهْتَدُوا بِهِ فَسَيَقُولُونَ هذا إِفْكٌ ** (And as they have not been guided by it, they will say, 'This is an ancient lie.') a lie (كذب) that is ancient (قَدِيمٌ). And it is like their saying: "tales (أساطير) of the ancients."

** وَقالَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا لِلَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لَوْ كانَ خَيْراً ما سَبَقُونا إِلَيْهِ وَإِذْ لَمْ يَهْتَدُوا بِهِ فَسَيَقُولُونَ هذا إِفْكٌ قَدِيمٌ ** (And the faithless said of the faithful: 'Had it been any good, they would not have preceded us to it.' And as they have not been guided by it, they will say, 'This is an ancient lie.')

(١٢) وَمِنْ قَبْلِهِ ** (12) And before it And before the Qur'an كِتابُ مُوسى إِماماً وَرَحْمَةً وَهذا كِتابٌ مُصَدِّقٌ ** was the Book of Moses, a guide and a mercy. And this is a confirming Book for the Book of Moses لِساناً عَرَبِيًّا لِيُنْذِرَ الَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا ** in an Arabic tongue, that it may warn those who do wrong And it was recited with the tāʾ (بالتّاء) وَبُشْرى لِلْمُحْسِنِينَ. and be good news for the virtuous.**

(١٣) إِنَّ الَّذِينَ قالُوا رَبُّنَا اللهُ ثُمَّ اسْتَقامُوا ** (13) Indeed, those who say, 'Our Lord is Allah,' and then remain steadfast, It was said that is, they combined al-Tawhid (التوحيد), which is the essence (خلاصة) of knowledge (العلم), and steadfastness (الاستقامة) in affairs (الأمور), which is the ultimate goal (منتهى) of action (العمل). And thumma (then) is to indicate the subordination (تأخّر) of the rank (رتبة) of action and the dependence (توقّف) of its validity (اعتباره) upon al-Tawhid. And al-Qummi said regarding اسْتَقامُوا ** (they remained steadfast): [It means steadfastness] upon the wilayah (ولاية) of the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him). And an explanation for this has already passed in Ḥā Mīm al-Sajdah. فَلا خَوْفٌ عَلَيْهِمْ ** (there shall be no fear for them) from the occurrence of something disliked (مكروه), وَلا هُمْ يَحْزَنُونَ ** (nor shall they grieve) over the loss of something beloved (محبوب).

** أُولئِكَ أَصْحابُ الْجَنَّةِ خالِدِينَ فِيها جَزاءً بِما كانُوا يَعْمَلُونَ. (14) They are the inhabitants of paradise, abiding therein, a reward for what they used to do. ** وَوَصَّيْنَا الْإِنْسانَ بِوالِدَيْهِ حُسْناً ** (15) We have enjoined man to be good to his parents. And it was recited (قُرِئَ) as إحساناً. In al-Majma', narrated from Ali (peace be upon him): (حُسْناً) with two fathahs. ** حَمَلَتْهُ أُمُّهُ كُرْهاً وَوَضَعَتْهُ كُرْهاً ** His mother carried him with difficulty, and gave birth to him with difficulty. And it was recited (قُرِئَ) with fath [i.e., karhan]. ** وَحَمْلُهُ وَفِصالُهُ ** and his carrying and his weaning Meaning: the duration of his carrying and his weaning. And it was recited (قُرِئَ) as وفصله. ** ثَلاثُونَ شَهْراً ** is thirty months. All of that is a clarification of what the mother endures in raising the child, [stated] as an emphasis in enjoining [goodness] toward her. ** حَتَّى إِذا بَلَغَ أَشُدَّهُ ** Until, when he comes of age Meaning: his strength and intellect have become firm. وَبَلَغَ أَرْبَعِينَ سَنَةً قالَ رَبِّ أَوْزِعْنِي and reaches forty years, he says, 'My Lord, inspire me Meaning: inspire me (أَلْهِمْنِي). ** أَنْ أَشْكُرَ نِعْمَتَكَ الَّتِي أَنْعَمْتَ عَلَيَّ وَعَلى والِدَيَّ وَأَنْ أَعْمَلَ صالِحاً تَرْضاهُ وَأَصْلِحْ لِي فِي ذُرِّيَّتِي إِنِّي تُبْتُ إِلَيْكَ ** to thank Your blessing which You have bestowed on me and on my parents, and to do righteous deeds which please You, and set right my progeny for me. Indeed, I have turned to You penitently, Meaning: concerning what distracts [me] from You. ** وَإِنِّي مِنَ الْمُسْلِمِينَ ** and I am one of the Muslims. Meaning: those sincere (الْمُخْلِصِينَ) to You.

(١٦) أُولئِكَ الَّذِينَ نَتَقَبَّلُ عَنْهُمْ أَحْسَنَ ما عَمِلُوا وَنَتَجاوَزُ عَنْ سَيِّئاتِهِمْ (16) Those are the ones from whom We accept the best of what they have done, and overlook their misdeeds. And it was recited with the ن (نَتَقَبَّلُ, نَتَجاوَزُ) in both instances. فِي أَصْحابِ الْجَنَّةِ وَعْدَ الصِّدْقِ الَّذِي كانُوا يُوعَدُونَ ** (They will be) among the inhabitants of Paradise—a true promise which they were promised** in the world.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: When Fatimah became pregnant with al-Husayn (peace be upon them both), Gabriel (peace be upon him) came to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and said: "Fatimah will give birth to a boy whom your nation will kill after you." When Fatimah became pregnant with al-Husayn (peace be upon them both), she disliked carrying Him, and when she gave birth to Him, she disliked His birth. Then He (Gabriel) said: "No mother in the world has ever given birth to a boy whom she disliked, but she disliked Him because she knew that He would be killed." He said: And concerning Him, this verse was revealed. And in another narration: Then Gabriel descended and said: "O Muhammad, your Lord sends His greetings to you and gives you the glad tidings that He is placing the Imamate (الإمامة), the Guardianship (الولاية), and the Successorship (الوصيّة) in His progeny." He (the Prophet) said: "Indeed, I am pleased." Then He gave Fatimah the glad tidings of that, and she was pleased. He said: "Had it not been that He (Allah) said, 'Set right for me in my progeny,' all of His progeny would have been Imams (أئمّة)." He said: Al-Husayn (peace be upon him) was not suckled by Fatimah nor by any female. He would be brought to the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, who would place His thumb in His mouth, and He would suckle from it what sufficed Him for two or three days. Thus, the flesh of al-Husayn (peace be upon him) grew from the flesh and blood of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. No one was born at six months except Jesus son of Mary (peace be upon him) and al-Husayn (peace be upon him).

In al-Ilal, narrated from Him (peace be upon him), is a narration close to it, and al-Qummi added to it and subtracted from it.

In Irshad al-Mufid, they narrated: That a woman who had given birth at six months was brought to Umar, and he intended to stone her. So the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) said to him: "If I argue with you using the Book of Allah, I will defeat you in argument. Indeed, Allah the Almighty says: وَحَمْلُهُ وَفِصالُهُ ثَلاثُونَ شَهْراً (and his carrying and his weaning are thirty months). He says: وَالْوالِداتُ يُرْضِعْنَ أَوْلادَهُنَّ حَوْلَيْنِ كامِلَيْنِ لِمَنْ أَرادَ أَنْ يُتِمَّ الرَّضاعَةَ ** (233) Mothers shall suckle their children for two full years—for such as desire to complete the suckling. (This refers to) two years. Since His carrying and His weaning were thirty months, the carrying period among them was six months." So Umar released the woman, and the ruling was established by this, and it has been acted upon by the Companions (الصحابة), the Successors (التابعون), and those who took from them until this day of ours.

In al-Khisal, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: When the servant reaches thirty-three years of age, he has reached his prime (أشدّه). And when he reaches forty years of age, he has reached and attained his limit (منتهاه). If he enters into forty-one, he is in decline (النقصان). And it is fitting for the one who is fifty to be like one who is in the throes of death (النّزع).

(١٧) وَالَّذِي قالَ لِوالِدَيْهِ أُفٍّ لَكُما أَتَعِدانِنِي ** (But as for him who says to his parents, 'Fie upon you two! Do you promise me) And it was read with a single nūn (نون) that is emphasized (أَتَعِدَانِّي) أَنْ أُخْرَجَ (that) I will be resurrected (أُبْعَث) وَقَدْ خَلَتِ الْقُرُونُ مِنْ قَبْلِي ** (when generations have passed before me?') and none of them returned وَهُما يَسْتَغِيثانِ اللهَ وَيْلَكَ آمِنْ إِنَّ وَعْدَ اللهِ حَقٌّ فَيَقُولُ ما هذا إِلَّا أَساطِيرُ الْأَوَّلِينَ ** (The two of them implore Allah for help, [saying,] 'Woe to you! Believe! Indeed, Allah's promise is true.' But he says, 'This is nothing but myths of the ancients.') their falsehoods (أَبَاطِيلُهُمْ) which they wrote down. Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) said: It was revealed concerning Abd al-Rahman ibn Abi Bakr (عبد الرحمن بن أبي بكر).

(١٨) أُولئِكَ الَّذِينَ حَقَّ عَلَيْهِمُ الْقَوْلُ ** (18) They are those against whom the word has become due, meaning that they are the inhabitants of the Fire, فِي أُمَمٍ قَدْ خَلَتْ مِنْ قَبْلِهِمْ مِنَ الْجِنِّ وَالْإِنْسِ إِنَّهُمْ كانُوا خاسِرِينَ. among the nations that have passed before them, of jinn and humans. Indeed, they have been losers. (١٩) وَلِكُلٍ ** (19) For everyone of the two parties دَرَجاتٌ ** there are ranks, degrees (مراتب), مِمَّا عَمِلُوا ** according to what they have done, from the recompense of what they did of good and evil, or because of what they did. And the degrees (الدرجات) are predominantly used for reward (المثوبة), but here they have come by way of generalization (التغليب). وَلِيُوَفِّيَهُمْ أَعْمالَهُمْ ** and that He may pay them fully for their deeds, their recompense (جزاءها). And it was read with the nūn (بالنون). وَهُمْ لا يُظْلَمُونَ ** and they will not be wronged. by a reduction in reward (ثواب) or an increase in punishment (عقاب).

(٢٠) ** وَيَوْمَ يُعْرَضُ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا عَلَى النَّارِ ** (20) And the day those who are faithless are exposed to the Fire they will be tormented by it. And it was said: The Fire will be exposed to them, and the phrasing is inverted for emphasis (مبالغة), like their saying: "The she-camel was exposed to the watering trough (الحوض)." ** أَذْهَبْتُمْ طَيِّباتِكُمْ ** your pleasures, meaning: it will be said to them, "You have exhausted [them]." And it was read with interrogation (i.e., أَذْهَبْتُمْ - Did you exhaust?) ** فِي حَياتِكُمُ الدُّنْيا ** by fully consuming them ** وَاسْتَمْتَعْتُمْ بِها ** so nothing remains of them for you.

Al-Qummi said: You ate, drank, wore, and rode, and they are [now] with so-and-so's children (بني فلان). ** فَالْيَوْمَ تُجْزَوْنَ عَذابَ الْهُونِ ** He said: [This means] thirst (العطش). ** بِما كُنْتُمْ تَسْتَكْبِرُونَ فِي الْأَرْضِ بِغَيْرِ الْحَقِّ وَبِما كُنْتُمْ تَفْسُقُونَ ** from obedience to Allah.

In al-Mahasin, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq, from his forefathers (peace be upon them), he said: A khabis (خبيص) was brought to the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, but He refused to eat it. It was said: "Do you forbid it?" He said: "No, but I dislike that my soul should long for it." Then He recited this Ayah: ** أَذْهَبْتُمْ طَيِّباتِكُمْ فِي حَياتِكُمُ الدُّنْيا. ** (You have exhausted your good things in your worldly life.)

(٢١) وَاذْكُرْ أَخا عادٍ ** (21) And mention the brother of 'Ad, meaning Hud, إِذْ أَنْذَرَ قَوْمَهُ بِالْأَحْقافِ ** when he warned his people in the Ahqaf. It was said that it is the plural of حقْف (hiqf), which is a long, elevated sand dune (رمل مستطيل مرتفع) containing a curve (انحناء). Al-Qummi (said): Al-Ahqaf are from the lands of 'Ad, extending from al-Shuquq to al-Ajfar, and they comprise four stopping places (منازل). وَقَدْ خَلَتِ النُّذُرُ ** while warners have passed (meaning: the Messengers (الرسل)) مِنْ بَيْنِ يَدَيْهِ وَمِنْ خَلْفِهِ ** before him and behind him, (meaning: before Hud and after him) أَلَّا تَعْبُدُوا إِلَّا اللهَ إِنِّي أَخافُ عَلَيْكُمْ عَذابَ يَوْمٍ عَظِيمٍ [saying:] 'Worship no one but Allah. Indeed, I fear for you the punishment of a tremendous day.' (meaning: terrible/awe-inspiring (هائل) due to your polytheism (شرككم)).

(٢٢) قالُوا أَجِئْتَنا لِتَأْفِكَنا ** meaning: to divert us (لتصرفنا) عَنْ آلِهَتِنا ** meaning: from their worship (عن عبادتها). فَأْتِنا بِما تَعِدُنا ** meaning: of the punishment (من العذاب) for polytheism (على الشرك). إِنْ كُنْتَ مِنَ الصَّادِقِينَ ** meaning: regarding your promise (في وعدك). (٢٢) They said, 'Have you come to us to turn us away from our gods? Then bring us what you promise us, if you are one of the truthful.'

(٢٣) قالَ إِنَّمَا الْعِلْمُ عِنْدَ اللهِ ** I have no knowledge (لا علم لي) of the time of your punishment (بوقت عذابكم), nor do I have any involvement (ولا مدخل لي) in hastening it (فيه فاستعجل به). Rather, the knowledge of it (وانّما علمه) is with Allah, so He will bring it upon you (فيأتيكم به) at the time decreed for Him (المقدّر له). And I convey to you what I have been sent with (وَأُبَلِّغُكُمْ ما أُرْسِلْتُ بِهِ), and the Messenger is only responsible for the conveyance (وما على الرسول الّا البلاغ). وَلكِنِّي أَراكُمْ قَوْماً تَجْهَلُونَ ** meaning: You do not know (لا تعلمون) that the Messengers were sent as conveyers (مبلغين) and warners (ومنذرين), not as punishers (لا معذّبين) who propose terms (مقترحين). (٢٣) He said, 'The knowledge [of its time] is only with Allah, and I convey to you what I have been sent with, but I see that you are a people who are ignorant.'

(٢٤) فَلَمَّا رَأَوْهُ عارِضاً ** (24) Then, when they saw it as a cloud advancing toward their valleys, they said, 'This is a cloud that will give us rain.' [Hud] said, 'Nay, it is what you sought to hasten: a wind in which there is a painful punishment.' a cloud that appeared across the horizon of the sky مُسْتَقْبِلَ أَوْدِيَتِهِمْ ** directed towards their valleys قالُوا هذا عارِضٌ مُمْطِرُنا ** meaning, it brings us rain بَلْ هُوَ ** meaning, Hud said بَلْ هُوَ مَا اسْتَعْجَلْتُمْ بِهِ ** of the punishment رِيحٌ ** it is a wind فِيها عَذابٌ أَلِيمٌ.

(٢٥) تُدَمِّرُ ** تهلك (It destroys/ruins) means: It annihilates. كُلَّ شَيْءٍ ** من نفوسهم وأموالهم (everything) means: from their souls and their wealth. بِأَمْرِ رَبِّها فَأَصْبَحُوا لا يُرى إِلَّا مَساكِنُهُمْ ** (by the command of its Lord. Then they became such that nothing was seen except their dwellings.) Meaning: So the wind came to them and destroyed them, and they became [ruined]. And it was recited (وقرئ) as لا ترى (you do not see) in the second person address (على الخطاب), meaning: such that if you were present in their lands, you would not see anything except their dwellings. And it was recited (وقرئ) as لا يري (He does not show/see) with a ḍamma on the yāʾ (بالياء المضمومة) and the word al-masākin (المساكن) in the nominative case (ورفع المساكن). كَذلِكَ نَجْزِي الْقَوْمَ الْمُجْرِمِينَ ** (Thus do We requite the guilty people.) Al-Qummi said: Their Prophet was Hūd, and their lands were abundant in goodness (كثيرة الخير) and fertile (خصبة). Then Allah withheld the rain from them for seven years until they suffered drought (اجدبوا) and their goodness departed from their lands. And Hūd used to say to them what Allah narrated in Sūrat Hūd: اسْتَغْفِرُوا رَبَّكُمْ ثُمَّ تُوبُوا إِلَيْهِ (Seek forgiveness from your Lord, then turn to Him penitently) up to His saying: وَلا تَتَوَلَّوْا مُجْرِمِينَ ** (and do not turn your backs as guilty ones.) But they did not believe and they rebelled (عتوا). So Allah revealed to Hūd that the punishment would come to them at such and such a time: رِيحٌ فِيها عَذابٌ أَلِيمٌ (a wind containing a painful punishment). When that time came, they looked towards a cloud that had approached, and they rejoiced. So قالُوا هذا عارِضٌ مُمْطِرُنا ** (They said, 'This is a cloud that will bring us rain.') [meaning:] Now we will receive rain. So Hūd said to them: بَلْ هُوَ مَا اسْتَعْجَلْتُمْ بِهِ ** (Rather, it is what you sought to hasten) up to His saying: بِأَمْرِ رَبِّها ** (by the command of its Lord). He said: Its wording (لفظه) is general (عامّ) but its meaning (معناه) is specific (خاصّ), because it left many things that it did not destroy. Rather, it only destroyed all of their wealth (مالهم كلّه). He said: And all these accounts of the destruction of nations are a warning (تخويف) and an admonition (تحذير) for the Ummah of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله. And it was narrated that when Hūd sensed the wind, he withdrew with the believers into the enclosure (الحظيرة). The wind came and tilted the sand dunes (الأحقاف) over the disbelievers, and they remained beneath them for seven nights and eight days. Then it uncovered them, carried them away, and cast them into the sea.

(٢٦) وَلَقَدْ مَكَّنَّاهُمْ فِيما إِنْ مَكَّنَّاكُمْ فِيهِ ** (26) Certainly We had established them in that wherein We have not established you, The particle إن (in) is either negating (نافية) or conditional (شرطيّة) with an omitted apodosis (محذوفة الجواب), meaning: your transgression (بغيكم) was greater. وَجَعَلْنا لَهُمْ سَمْعاً وَأَبْصاراً وَأَفْئِدَةً ** and We gave them hearing, sight, and hearts. So that they might recognize those blessings, and deduce through them concerning the Giver of them, and persist in His gratitude. فَما أَغْنى عَنْهُمْ سَمْعُهُمْ وَلا أَبْصارُهُمْ وَلا أَفْئِدَتُهُمْ مِنْ شَيْءٍ ** Yet their hearing, sight, and hearts did not avail them in any way, in terms of availing (من الإغناء). إِذْ كانُوا يَجْحَدُونَ بِآياتِ اللهِ وَحاقَ بِهِمْ ما كانُوا بِهِ يَسْتَهْزِؤُنَ ** since they used to impugn the signs of Allah, and they were besieged by what they used to ridicule. (This refers to) the punishment (من العذاب). In al-Qummi: We had given them, but they disbelieved, so the punishment descended upon them. So beware lest there descends upon you what descended upon them.

(٢٧) وَلَقَدْ أَهْلَكْنا ما حَوْلَكُمْ ** O people of Mecca مِنَ الْقُرى ** like حجر ثمود, and it was read (قُرِئَ) (as the towns of) the people of Lot وَصَرَّفْنَا الْآياتِ ** by repeating them لَعَلَّهُمْ يَرْجِعُونَ ** from their disbelief. (27) And certainly We destroyed what was around you of the towns, and We varied the signs so that they might return.

(٢٨) فَلَوْ لا نَصَرَهُمُ الَّذِينَ اتَّخَذُوا مِنْ دُونِ اللهِ قُرْباناً آلِهَةً ** Then why did their gods, whom they use to seek proximity to Allah, not prevent them from destruction, since they said, "These are our intercessors with Allah"? بَلْ ضَلُّوا عَنْهُمْ ** Rather, they were absent from helping them, and it became impossible for them to seek aid through them, an impossibility of seeking aid through misguidance (الضلال). وَذلِكَ إِفْكُهُمْ ** And that taking (of gods), whose effect is this, turned them away from the truth (الحقّ) وَما كانُوا يَفْتَرُونَ.** (28) Why did not those whom they took as gods besides Allah, seeking proximity [to Him], help them? Rather, they failed them. And that was their lie and what they used to fabricate.

(٢٩) وَإِذْ صَرَفْنا إِلَيْكَ نَفَراً مِنَ الْجِنِ ** (29) And when We directed toward you a company of jinn We made them incline toward you (أَمَلْنَاهُمْ إِلَيْكَ). And the nafar (النَّفَر) is less than ten.

And in al-Ihtijaj, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): that they were nine: one was from the jinn of Nasibin (نَصِيبِينَ) and eight were from Bani 'Amr ibn 'Amir (بَنِي عَمْرِو بْنِ عَامِر), and He mentioned their names. يَسْتَمِعُونَ الْقُرْآنَ فَلَمَّا حَضَرُوهُ قالُوا أَنْصِتُوا ** listening to the Quran. When they were present at it, they said, 'Be silent!' Some of them said to others: Be silent so that we may listen to it. فَلَمَّا قُضِيَ ** Then, when it was finished, it was completed and finished from its recitation, **وَلَّوْا إِلى قَوْمِهِمْ مُنْذِرِينَ ** they returned to their people as warners. warning them.

(٣٠) قالُوا يا قَوْمَنا إِنَّا سَمِعْنا كِتاباً أُنْزِلَ مِنْ بَعْدِ مُوسى مُصَدِّقاً لِما بَيْنَ يَدَيْهِ يَهْدِي إِلَى الْحَقِّ وَإِلى طَرِيقٍ مُسْتَقِيمٍ. (30) They said, 'O our people! We have heard a Book that was sent down after Moses, confirming what was before it, guiding to the truth and to a straight path.' (٣١) يا قَوْمَنا أَجِيبُوا داعِيَ اللهِ وَآمِنُوا بِهِ يَغْفِرْ لَكُمْ مِنْ ذُنُوبِكُمْ ** (31) O our people! Answer the caller of Allah and believe in Him. He will forgive you some of your sins "some of your sins" (بعض ذنوبكم). It was said that this refers to what pertains purely to the right of Allah (خالص حقّ الله), for injustices (المظالم) are not forgiven merely by faith (بالايمان). وَيُجِرْكُمْ مِنْ عَذابٍ أَلِيمٍ ** and protect you from a painful punishment.' prepared for the disbelievers (للكفّار).

(٣٢) وَمَنْ لا يُجِبْ داعِيَ اللهِ فَلَيْسَ بِمُعْجِزٍ فِي الْأَرْضِ ** (32) And whoever does not answer the summoner of Allah, cannot frustrate [Him] on the earth, since no escape can save him from Him, وَلَيْسَ لَهُ مِنْ دُونِهِ أَوْلِياءُ to protect him from Him. أُولئِكَ فِي ضَلالٍ مُبِينٍ ** Those are in manifest error. where they turned away from answering the call of one whose status is such. Al-Qummiyy (القمّيّ): All of this is the narration of the Jinn. The reason for the revelation of this verse was that the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله went out from Mecca to the market of Ukaz (عكاظ) accompanied by Zayd ibn Harithah, inviting people to Islam, but no one answered Him, and He found no one who would accept Him. Then He returned to Mecca. When He reached a place called Wadi Majannah (وادي مجنّة), He performed the night vigil (تهجّد) reciting the Quran in the depth of the night. A group (نفر) of the Jinn passed by Him, and when they heard His recitation, some of them said to others: أَنْصِتُوا ** Be silent, (meaning: be quiet). فَلَمَّا قُضِيَ ** So when it was finished, (meaning: when the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله finished the recitation) وَلَّوْا إِلى قَوْمِهِمْ مُنْذِرِينَ They said: O our people, up to His saying: فِي ضَلالٍ مُبِينٍ So they came to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله, embraced Islam, and believed. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله taught them the laws (شرايع) of Islam. Then Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, revealed to His Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله: قُلْ أُوحِيَ إِلَيَّ أَنَّهُ اسْتَمَعَ نَفَرٌ مِنَ الْجِنِ ** Say, ‘It has been revealed to me that a group of jinn listened [to the Qur’an].’ (the entire Surah). Thus, Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, narrated their saying, and the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله appointed a leader (ولّى) over them from among themselves. They used to return to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله at all times, so the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله commanded the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) to teach them and give them understanding (يفقّههم). Among them are believers (مؤمنون), disbelievers (كافرون), Nasibis (ناصبون), Jews (يهود), Christians (نصارى), and Magians (مجوس), and they are the offspring of al-Jann (الجانّ). The Scholar (العالم) (peace be upon him) was asked about the believing Jinn (مؤمني الجنّ): "Will they enter Paradise (الجنّة)?" He replied: "No. But Allah has enclosures (خطائر) between Paradise (الجنّة) and the Fire (النار), where the believing Jinn (مؤمن الجنّ) and the transgressors (فسّاق) among the Shi'ah (الشيعة) will be."

(٣٣) أَوَلَمْ يَرَوْا أَنَّ اللهَ الَّذِي خَلَقَ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضَ وَلَمْ يَعْيَ بِخَلْقِهِنَّ بِقادِرٍ عَلى أَنْ يُحْيِيَ الْمَوْتى ** (33) Do they not see that Allah, who created the heavens and the earth and was not weary of creating them, is able to revive the dead? The bāʾ (الباء) is added to emphasize the negation. And it was recited (وقرئ) as بِقَدَرٍ. بَلى إِنَّهُ عَلى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ.** Yes, indeed He is able to do all things.

(٣٤) وَيَوْمَ يُعْرَضُ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا عَلَى النَّارِ أَلَيْسَ هذا بِالْحَقِ ** (34) The day the faithless are exposed to the Fire: 'Is this not the truth?' The reference is to the punishment. قالُوا بَلى وَرَبِّنا قالَ فَذُوقُوا الْعَذابَ بِما كُنْتُمْ تَكْفُرُونَ (They will say: 'Yes, by our Lord!' He will say: 'Taste the punishment because of what you used to disbelieve.') This is an insult (إهانة) and a rebuke (توبيخ) for them.

(٣٥) فَاصْبِرْ كَما صَبَرَ أُولُوا الْعَزْمِ مِنَ الرُّسُلِ ** (35) So be patient, just as the resolute among the apostles were patient. They are those among them possessing steadfastness (الثّبات) and diligence (الجدّ), and you are one of them. And أولوا العزم (the resolute) are the possessors of the Divine Laws (الشرايع) who strove diligently in establishing and confirming them, and were patient regarding their hardships (مشاقّها).

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding this Ayah, he said: "They are Noah, Abraham, Moses, Jesus, and Muhammad (peace be upon him and his progeny, and peace be upon them all)." He was asked: "How did they become أولي العزم (the resolute)?" He replied: "Because Noah was sent with a Book (كتاب) and a Divine Law (شريعة). Everyone who came after Noah (peace be upon him) followed his Divine Law and methodology (منهاجه) until Abraham (peace be upon him) came with the Scrolls (الصحف) and with the resolve (عزيمة) to leave Noah's Book, not out of disbelief in it. Every Prophet who came after Abraham (peace be upon him) followed Abraham's Divine Law, his methodology, and the Scrolls, until Moses came with the Torah (التوراة), his Divine Law, and his methodology, and with the resolve to leave the Scrolls. Every Prophet who came after Moses (peace be upon him) followed the Torah, his Divine Law, and his methodology, until the Messiah (peace be upon him) came with the Gospel (الإنجيل) and with the resolve to leave Moses' Divine Law and methodology. Every Prophet who came after the Messiah followed his Divine Law and methodology, until Muhammad (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him and his progeny) came. He came with the Quran, his Divine Law, and his methodology. Thus, what He made lawful (حلاله) is lawful until the Day of Resurrection, and what He made unlawful (حرامه) is unlawful until the Day of Resurrection. These are أولو العزم من الرسل (the resolute among the apostles)."

And narrated from him (peace be upon him): The masters (سادة) of the Prophets are five, and they are أُولُوا الْعَزْمِ مِنَ الرُّسُلِ ** (the resolute among the apostles). The millstone (الرّحا) revolved around them: Noah, Abraham, Moses, Jesus, and Muhammad (may the blessings of Allah be upon him and his progeny, and upon them and upon all the Prophets).

In al-Uyun, narrated from Imam al-Rida (peace be upon him): [A narration] close to the two narrations.

In al-Kafi and al-Ilal, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): They were only named أولي العزم (the resolute) because a covenant (عهد) was taken from them concerning Muhammad (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him and his progeny), the Successors (الأوصياء) after him, and the Mahdi and his conduct (peace be upon them). So, their resolve (عزمهم) was united that this was indeed the case and they affirmed it.

And al-Qummi [said]: The meaning of أولي العزم (the resolute) is that they preceded the Prophets in affirming Allah and affirming every Prophet who came before them and after them, and they resolved (عزموا) to be patient despite the denial (التكذيب) and the harm (الأذى).

وَلا تَسْتَعْجِلْ لَهُمْ (And do not seek to hasten [the punishment] for them) refers to the disbelievers of Quraysh (كفّار قريش) with the punishment, for it will inevitably descend upon them at its appointed time. كَأَنَّهُمْ يَوْمَ يَرَوْنَ ما يُوعَدُونَ لَمْ يَلْبَثُوا إِلَّا ساعَةً مِنْ نَهارٍ ** (It will be as if, the day they see what they are promised, they had not remained [in the world] except for an hour of a day.) They will deem the duration of their stay in the world short due to the horror (هوله) [of the punishment], so much so that they will reckon it to be [only] an hour. بَلاغٌ ** (A proclamation.) This with which you have been admonished is sufficient (كفاية), or it is a conveyance (تبليغ) from the Messenger. فَهَلْ يُهْلَكُ إِلَّا الْقَوْمُ الْفاسِقُونَ ** (Will anyone be destroyed except the transgressing lot?) Those who deviate (الخارجون) from taking heed (الاتعاظ) and obedience (الطاعة).

In Thawab al-A'mal and al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "Whoever recites Surat al-Ahqaf every night or every Friday, Allah the Almighty will not afflict him with terror (روعة) in the life of this world, and He will secure him from the great terror (فزع) of the Day of Resurrection, if Allah wills."
